whatItMeasures: Platelets (thrombocytes) are small blood cells produced in the bone marrow that clump together to form clots and stop bleeding; the platelet count measures how many are present in a volume of blood.
causesOfHigh: Reactive thrombocytosis (most common) — infection, inflammation, iron deficiency, post-surgery
Post-splenectomy (the spleen normally removes old platelets)
causesOfLow: Immune thrombocytopenic purpura (ITP) — most common cause of isolated low platelets
Bone marrow failure (aplastic anaemia, myelodysplastic syndrome, leukaemia)

high: High platelets often cause no symptoms
In rare cases at very high counts: blood clots, stroke, or heart attack
Headache, dizziness, and burning pain in hands and feet (in essential thrombocythaemia)
low: Tiny red or purple dots on skin (petechiae)
Prolonged bleeding from cuts or after procedures
Spontaneous bleeding into joints or muscles (in severe cases)

citations.title: Immune Thrombocytopenia
citations.source: National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute (NHLBI)
